assertion_results.text: Step 7 Case A creates standard remediation tasks for the current stream (rhtpa-2.2) with standard labels ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123'] and 'Depend' link to TC-8020
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md, Case A creates two standard tasks for rhtpa-2.2: (1) Upstream Backport Task with labels ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123"] and a 'Depend' link to TC-8020 (lines 18-76), and (2) Downstream Propagation Subtask with the same labels and 'Depend' link to TC-8020 (lines 78-149). The summary table on lines 317-318 confirms: both rhtpa-2.2 tasks are 'Standard (Case A)' with labels 'ai-generated-jira, Security, CVE-2026-55123' and link type 'Depend'.

assertion_results.text: Step 7 Case B detects that stream rhtpa-2.1 is also affected but has no CVE Jira — creates proactive preemptive remediation tasks (§1.60)
assertion_results.evidence: In version-impact.md (lines 26-29): 'A JQL search for sibling CVE Jiras with label CVE-2026-55123 returns **no results** for stream rhtpa-2.1 -- no CVE Jira exists for that stream. This triggers Step 7 Case B (preemptive remediation).' In cross-stream.md (lines 30-31): 'Result: No sibling Vulnerability issues found for CVE-2026-55123 in stream rhtpa-2.1.' and (lines 36-37): 'Since stream rhtpa-2.1 is affected but has no CVE Jira, Step 7 Case B applies. Proactive preemptive remediation tasks are created for this stream.' In remediation.md (lines 153-155): 'Stream rhtpa-2.1 is also affected (tokio 1.40.0 < 1.42.0) but has **no CVE Jira** for this vulnerability... Per Step 7 Case B, proactive preemptive remediation tasks are created.' Two preemptive tasks are created: upstream backport (lines 157-231) and downstream propagation (lines 233-303).

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks for stream rhtpa-2.1 include the 'security-preemptive' label alongside standard labels: ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123', 'security-preemptive']
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md, Preemptive Task 1 (line 170): labels: ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123", "security-preemptive"]. Preemptive Task 2 (line 246): labels: ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123", "security-preemptive"]. The summary table (lines 319-320) confirms both preemptive tasks carry all four labels including 'security-preemptive'. Cross-stream.md (lines 44, 58) also confirms the same label set.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks are linked to the originating CVE Jira TC-8020 with 'Related' link type (not 'Depend') because the originating CVE belongs to a different stream
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md, Preemptive Task 1 post-creation actions (lines 224-231): 'Link to TC-8020 with "Related" link type (NOT "Depend", because the originating CVE belongs to a different stream)' with jira.create_link type: "Related". Preemptive Task 2 (lines 298-302): same 'Related' link type to TC-8020. The summary table (line 320) confirms link type is 'Related' for both preemptive tasks. Cross-stream.md (lines 45, 59): 'Link to TC-8020: "Related" (not "Depend") -- because TC-8020 belongs to a different stream (rhtpa-2.2)'. The comparison table in cross-stream.md (line 87) explicitly contrasts: Case A uses 'Depend', Case B uses 'Related'.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive task descriptions include a preemptive remediation prefix noting TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2) as the originating CVE
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md, Preemptive Task 1 description (lines 184-188): '> **Preemptive remediation**: This task was created proactively from cross-stream > impact analysis of TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2). > No stream-specific CVE Jira exists yet for this stream. When PSIRT creates one, > this task will be linked and the `security-preemptive` label removed.' Preemptive Task 2 description (lines 260-264) contains the identical preemptive remediation prefix. Cross-stream.md (lines 47-50, 61-64) repeats the same prefix content. Both explicitly name TC-8020 and stream rhtpa-2.2 as the originating CVE.
